It also authorises the application of certain further sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 1992 and reduces certain sums already authorised to be applied as appropriations in aid.

items.title: The Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1992
items.description: Parts II to X make fresh provision in relation to trade unions and employers' associations in place of the Trade Union Acts (Northern Ireland) 1871 to 1965 and certain provisions of the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Orders 1976, 1982 and 1987. Part II provides for the definition, status and general regulation of trade unions and employers' associations. Part III provides for legal proceedings and restrictions on legal liability. Part IV sets out certain rights of trade union members. Part V requires employers to disclose certain information to independent trade unions for the purposes of collective bargaining. Parts VI and VII provide for secret ballots of the members of trade unions before undertaking industrial action and for the purposes of elections for certain positions in the union. Part VIII restricts the application of funds by a trade union or employers' association for political purposes. Part IX provides for the appointment and functions of the Northern Ireland Certification Officer and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for the Rights of Trade Union Members. Part X provides for the amalgamation of trade unions or employers' associations.

items.title: The Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Order 1992

items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive No. 89/667/EEC on single-member private limited-liability companies (O.J. No. L395, 30.12.1989, p. 40). The Directive requires Member States to provide for the formation of a company having one member and to permit a company to be a single-member company, subject to certain safeguards. In relation to the United Kingdom it applies to private companies limited by shares or by guarantee.

No. L222, 14.8.1978, pages 11 to 31) (the Fourth E.C. Company Law Directive) and Directive 83/349/EEC on consolidated accounts (O.J. No. L193, 18.7.1983, pages 1 to 17) (the Seventh E.C. items.description: This Order brings into operation on 30th November 1992 Articles 9 and 11(1)(d) of the Education Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 1989.Article 9 requires that grant-aided schools may not provide courses of study for pupils of compulsory school age which lead to qualifications authenticated by a person other than a member of staff of the school, unless the qualification, and either the syllabus or syllabus criteria are approved by the Department.Article 11 (1)(d) requires Boards of Governors and Principals of grant-aided schools to secure that Article 9 is not contravened.This Order also brings into operation on 30th November 1992 the repeal of Article 13 of the 1987 Order.This Order also brings into operation on 1st April 1993 Article 56 of the 1989 Order which requires Education and Library Boards to make available a sum of money to Boards of Governors of special schools not covered by the scheme.

items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 90/428/EEC (O.J. No. L224, 18.8.90, p. 60) on trade in equidae intended for competition and laying down the conditions for participation therein. Subject to specified exceptions, (regulation 3), discrimination in competition on grounds of registration or origin in another Member State is prohibited (regulation 4). If a horse from another Member State is not permitted to compete, reasons must be given in writing to its owner or his authorised representative (regulation 5).

The Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992
items.description: These regulations provide for various matters which affect entitlement to disability working allowance. Regulations 3 and 4 and Schedule 1 concern the disabilities which place the claimant at a disadvantage in getting a job and the declaration of such disability by the claimant. Regulations 5 and 6 prescribe the circumstances in which a person is treated as being in Northern Ireland and provide for 16 hours work a week as a minimum requirement. Regulation 7 sets out the qualifying benefits for entitlement to disability working allowance. Regulations 8 to 11 make provision in respect of children and young persons who are normally living with the claimant as members of the household and the circumstances in which a person is to be treated as no longer being a member of the household.

items.title: City of Bristol (Portishead Docks) Act 1992

items.title: Folkestone Harbour Act 1992
items.description: An Act to empower Folkestone Properties Limited to reclaim land and construct works at Folkestone Harbour; to make provision with respect to dredging and moorings; and for connected or other purposes.

items.title: Stamp Duty (Temporary Provisions) Act 1992 (repealed 27.7.1999)
items.description: An Act to make provision conferring temporary relief from stamp duty and provision for payments by the Commissioners of Inland Revenue in respect of instruments already stamped.

items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with Finance.

items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1992 and 31st March 1993, to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament, and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: Access to Neighbouring Land Act 1992
items.description: An Act to enable persons who desire to carry out works to any land which are reasonably necessary for the preservation of that land to obtain access to neighbouring land in order to do so; and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Offshore Safety (Protection Against Victimisation) Act 1992 (repealed 30.8.1993)

items.title: Tourism (Overseas Promotion) (Wales) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to enable the Wales Tourist Board to carry on abroad activities to promote tourism to and within Wales.

items.title: Parliamentary Corporate Bodies Act 1992
items.description: An Act to establish corporate bodies to hold land and perform other functions for the benefit of the Houses of Parliament; to make provision for and in connection with the transfer of certain property, rights and liabilities to those corporate bodies; and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Medicinal Products: Prescription by Nurses etc. Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to medicinal products prescribed or otherwise ordered by registered nurses, midwives and health visitors.

items.title: Still-Birth (Definition) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to amend the law in respect of the definition of still-birth; to make certain consequential amendments of the law; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Severn Bridges Act 1992
items.description: An Act to provide for the construction of a new bridge over the Severn Estuary between England and Wales and roads leading to the new bridge and associated works; to make provision for the levying of tolls in respect of use of the existing Severn bridge and the new bridge; to make other provision for and in connection with the operation of the bridges; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Traffic Calming Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision about the carrying out on highways of works affecting the movement of vehicular and other traffic for the purposes of promoting safety and of preserving or improving the environment; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Firearms (Amendment) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to empower the Secretary of State to extend the period for which firearm and shot gun certificates are granted or renewed.

items.title: Cheques Act 1992
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to cheques.

items.title: Social Security (Mortgage Interest Payments) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision for requiring, in certain cases where interest on a loan secured on land is payable by a person who is entitled, or whose partner, former partner or qualifying associate is entitled, to income support, the applicable amount in respect of which includes a sum in respect of that interest, that a part of the benefits to which any of those persons is entitled under the enactments relating to social security shall be paid directly to the lender and applied towards the discharge of the liability in respect of the interest; and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to anonymity in connection with allegations of, and criminal proceedings relating to, certain sexual offences.

items.title: Timeshare Act 1992 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to provide for rights to cancel certain agreements about timeshare accommodation.

items.title: Sea Fisheries (Wildlife Conservation) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to require appropriate Ministers and relevant bodies to have regard to the conservation of flora and fauna in the discharge of their functions under the Sea Fisheries Acts.

items.title: Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make new provision about further and higher education in Scotland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Education (Schools) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the inspection of schools and with respect to information about schools and their pupils.

items.title: Army Act 1992 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to provide for members of the Ulster Defence Regiment to cease to be members of that Regiment at the end of June 1992; to provide for the amendment of section 2 of the Armed Forces Act 1966 in relation to service in Northern Ireland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to social security contributions and benefits with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Friendly Societies Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make further provision for friendly societies; to provide for the cessation of registration under the Friendly Societies Act 1974; to make provision about disputes involving friendly societies or other bodies registered under the Friendly Societies Act 1974 and about the functions of the Chief Registrar of friendly societies; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Charities Act 1992
items.description: An Act to amend the Charities Act 1960 and make other provision with respect to charities; to regulate fund-raising activities carried on in connection with charities and other institutions; to make fresh provision with respect to public charitable collections; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Transport and Works Act 1992
items.description: An Act to provide for the making of orders relating to, or to matters ancillary to, the construction or operation of railways, tramways, trolley vehicle systems, other guided transport systems and inland waterways, and orders relating to, or to matters ancillary to, works interfering with rights of navigation; to make further provision in relation to railways, tramways, trolley vehicle systems and other guided transport systems; to amend certain enactments relating to harbours; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Competition and Service (Utilities) Act 1992
Museums and Galleries Act 1992
items.description: An Act to establish Boards of Trustees of the National Gallery, the Tate Gallery, the National Portrait Gallery and the Wallace Collection; to transfer property to them and confer functions on them; to make new provision as to transfers to and between the collections of certain museums, galleries and libraries; to make provision for and in connection with the vesting of land in the governing bodies of such institutions; to make provision for the financing of such institutions and of the Museums and Galleries Commission; to make further provision with respect to the giving of indemnities against the loss of, or damage to, objects on loan to certain institutions; to change the name of, and to make further provision with respect to, the British Museum (Natural History); and to amend certain enactments relating to museums, galleries and libraries; and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Mauritius Republic Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision in connection with Mauritius becoming a republic within the Commonwealth.

items.title: Non-Domestic Rating Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to non-domestic rating for the period beginning with 11th March 1992 and ending with 31st March 1995; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Appropriation (No. 2) Act 1992 (repealed 21.7.1994)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending on 31st March 1993 and to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament.

items.title: Finance (No. 2) Act 1992
Community Care (Residential Accommodation) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to the making of arrangements under section 21 of the National Assistance Act 1948 for the provision of residential accommodation in premises managed by persons other than local authorities.

items.title: Social Security Administration Act 1992
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to the administration of social security and related matters with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Carriage of Goods by Sea Act 1992
items.description: An Act to replace the Bills of Lading Act 1855 with new provision with respect to bills of lading and certain other shipping documents.

items.title: Protection of Badgers Act 1992
Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992
Tribunals and Inquiries Act 1992
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Tribunals and Inquiries Act 1971 and certain other enactments relating to tribunals and inquiries.

items.title: Human Fertilisation and Embryology (Disclosure of Information) Act 1992 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to relax the restrictions on the disclosure of information imposed by section 33(5) of the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990.

items.title: Boundary Commissions Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to the membership of the Boundary Commissions, the timing of their reports and the local government boundaries of which account is to be taken in their reports.

items.title: Maintenance Orders (Reciprocal Enforcement) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to amend the Maintenance Orders (Facilities for Enforcement) Act 1920 and the Maintenance Orders (Reciprocal Enforcement) Act 1972; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sporting Events (Control of Alcohol etc.) (Amendment) Act 1992 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to extend the maximum period for which an order under section 3 of the Sporting Events (Control of Alcohol etc.) Act 1985 may remain in force.

items.title: Car Tax (Abolition) Act 1992 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to abolish car tax.

items.title: Consolidated Fund (No. 3) Act 1992 (repealed 19.7.1995)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1993 and 1994.

items.title: Social Security (Consequential Provisions) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision for repeals, consequential amendments, transitional and transitory matters and savings in connection with the consolidation of enactments in the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 and the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (including provisions to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission).

items.title: Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to licences under sections 4 and 4A of the Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1967.

items.title: Civil Service (Management Functions) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to functions relating to the management of Her Majesty’s Home Civil Service; and to make provision about parliamentary procedure in relation to legislation for Northern Ireland making corresponding provision with respect to the Northern Ireland Civil Service.

items.title: Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992
Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992
items.description: An Act to consolidate for Northern Ireland certain enactments relating to the administration of social security and related matters, with corrections and minor improvements under the Consolidation of Enactments (Procedure) Act 1949.

items.title: Social Security (Consequential Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Act 1992
The North Hull Housing Action Trust (Transfer of Property) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Building Societies) (Audit Powers) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 477A(1) and (2) and 482A of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 and come into force on 30th January 1992. They provide firstly for the furnishing of information to, and inspection of documents by, the Board in connection with payments of dividends and interest made by building societies without deduction of tax (“gross payments”) pursuant to regulation 4 of the Income Tax (Building Societies) (Dividends and Interest) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2231) (“the Building Societies Regulations”), other than payments falling within regulation 4(1)(n) of those Regulations.

items.title: The Liquor Licensing (Fees) (Scotland) Variation Order 1992
items.description: This Order varies the Liquor Licensing (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1990 which determines the fees payable by an applicant to a licensing board under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 in respect of matters listed in the Schedule to that Order.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) Order 1992
items.description: This Order specifies agreements, and a description of agreements, relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity in Northern Ireland to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”) is deemed not to apply and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100(1) of the Electricity Act 1989, electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act and therefore agreements relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity may fall within the scope of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that the agreements specified in Part I of the Schedule to the Order, together with agreements meeting the description specified in Part II of the Schedule and satisfying the condition specified therein, will nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Designation of Institutions of Higher Education (Scotland) Order 1992
The Social Security (Claims and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 ( “the Claims and Payments Regulations”)and are consequential upon the coming into force of the Social Security (Mortgage Interest Payments) Act 1992 (c. 33) ( “the 1992 Act”). The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of that Act and the regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24), Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from the requirement under section 10 of the Social Security Act 1980 (c. 30) to refer the proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Limited Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 3 Regulations 1992
The Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 9 Regulations 1992
The A40 Trunk Road (Sandhills) Detrunking Order 1992
The M1 Motorway (Junction 1 Improvement) Connecting Road Scheme 1992
The Seed Potatoes (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1991. They modify the tolerances for tobacco veinal necrosis virus infection in crops inspected with a view to classification as basic seed potatoes. The infection will no longer be separately identified and the tolerances for such infection will be determined by symptom expression (regulation 2(3)). They also correct a drafting error in regulation 13 of the 1991 Regulations.

items.title: The Teignmouth Harbour Revision Order 1992
The Petty Sessional Divisions (Humberside) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Employments) (No. 22) Regulations 1992
items.description: The Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1973 (“the PAYE Regulations ”) (as amended) allow certain employers to pay deductions under Pay As You Earn (  “PAYE ”) to the Inland Revenue quarterly rather than monthly. The employers in question are those whose average monthly payments of PAYE, National Insurance Contributions (  “NIC ”) and the statutory deduction imposed by section 559 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 on payments to sub-contractors are expected to be less than £400 in total.

items.title: The Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: The Income Tax (Sub-Contractors in the Construction Industry) Regulations 1975 (as amended) allow certain contractors to pay the deduction imposed by section 559 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 on payments to sub-contractors (“the statutory deduction”) to the Inland Revenue quarterly rather than monthly. The contractors in question are those whose average monthly payments of Pay As You Earn (“PAYE”), National Insurance Contributions (“NIC”) and the statutory deduction are expected to be less than £400 in total.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1992
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1991 (S.I. 1991/1898). Fees in respect of a few matters have been increased, namely those shown in relation to Forms TM3, TM6, TM10, TM11 and TM32, by not more than 10 on current fees.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Adoption of Children) (Amendment) 1992
Act of Sederunt (Applications under Part III of the Criminal Justice (International Co-operation) Act 1990) 1992
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for—

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Order 1992 as follows:—

items.title: The Relevant Population (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Combined Probation Areas (Humberside) Order 1992
The Companies Act 1985 (Welsh Language Accounts) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend Part VII of the Companies Act 1985 so as to enable the directors of a Welsh private company to deliver to the registrar of companies copies of accounts, directors' reports, audit reports and certain other accounting documents in Welsh without annexing an English translation. Where such documents are delivered in Welsh only, the Regulations require the registrar of companies to obtain and register a translation of the documents into English.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Amendment) (No.2) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1986, as amended, (  “the principal Order ”) which makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and obligations concerning technical measures for the conservation of fishery resources which are contained in Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3094/86, as previously amended. The principal Order creates offences in respect of breaches of the provisions of that Council Regulation referred to in column 1 (and briefly described in column 2) of Schedule 1 to that Order and in article 3(2) of that Order. The principal Order also specifies penalties for such offences and confers powers of enforcement.

items.title: The Seeds (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seeds (Fees) Regulations 1985, by prescribing revised fees in respect of matters arising under the Cereal Seeds Regulations 1985 (as amended), the Fodder Plant Seeds Regulations 1985 (as amended), the Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds Regulations 1985 (as amended), the Beet Seeds Regulations 1985 (as amended), the Vegetable Seeds Regulations 1985 (as amended) and the Seeds (Registration, Licensing and Enforcement) Regulations 1985 (as amended) (regulation 2).

items.title: The Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements, School Records and School Curriculum) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) Regulations 1990 by adding to the information which the head teacher of a school to which those regulations apply is required to make available to pupils' parents (regulation 2).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No. 13 Partial Revocation) Order 1992
The Kent County Council (Royal Military Canal Bridge—Hamstreet) Scheme 1990 Confirmation Instrument 1992
The Paisley College of Technology Scheme (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations expand the purposes for which the governing body are to maintain Paisley College of Technology to cover education and instruction in technology and science, business studies and social sciences and related subjects and also clarify the powers of the governing body.

items.title: The Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Spray Irrigation (Definition) Order 1992
items.description: This Order re-enacts with amendments the Spray Irrigation (Definition) Order 1965. The Order omits references to provisions in the Water Resources Act 1963 that are spent, and substitutes references to the Water Resources Act 1991, for provisions of the 1963 Act that have been re-enacted.

items.title: The A19 Trunk Road (Easingwold Bypass) Order 1992
The A19 Trunk Road (Raskelf Bridge to Shires Bridge) (Detrunking) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Building Societies) (Dividends and Interest) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 477A (1) and (2) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 and come into force on 30th January 1992, amend the Income Tax (Building Societies) (Dividends and Interest) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2231) (“the Building Societies Regulations”). In addition to making changes of a drafting nature, the amendments extend the categories of payments which may be made without deduction of tax by building societies and the categories of persons who can sign certificates and declarations. The amendments also exclude from the ambit of the Regulations payments in respect of quoted securities and provide for the furnishing of information in connection with payments of dividends and interest made by building societies.

items.title: The Education (Financial Delegation for Primary Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Income-related Benefits Schemes and Social Security (Recoupment) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989, the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991, the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 to provide that in calculating a claimant’s income and capital for determining his entitlement to those benefits, payments made in accordance with the provisions of a scheme established by the Secretary of State on 24th April 1992 and in Scotland, on 10th April 1992, for the benefit of certain persons, are to be disregarded.

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order 1992
The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Regulations 1988, which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the National Health Service Act 1977 and for the payment of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital. The time limit for making claims for repayment is extended from one month to three months (regulation 2). The extended time limit applies only in respect of charges paid or travelling expenses incurred on or after 1st June 1992 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No. 14 Revocation) Order 1992
The Cholsey and Wallingford Light Railway (Extension and Amendment) Order 1992
The A23 Trunk Road (Purley Way, Croydon) (Prohibition of U-Turns) Order 1992
The Merger Situation (Medicopharma NV and AAH Holdings plc) (Interim Provision) Order 1992
items.description: This Order—

items.title: The Social Security Commissioners Procedure (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Commissioners Procedure Regulations 1987 by extending the definition of “appeal tribunal” to include a disability appeal tribunal constituted in accordance with Schedule 10A to the Social Security Act 1975. Schedule 10A was inserted into the Social Security Act 1975 by paragraph 16 of Schedule 1 to the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (c. 21).

items.title: The Imported Food (Safeguards against Paralytic Toxin) (Pectinidae from Japan) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the importation into Great Britain of bivalve molluscs of the pectinidae family originating in Japan (whether dead or alive), and any products thereof, (regulation 2).

items.title: The First Community National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the First Community National Health Service Trust on 5th June 1992 trust property formerly held by Mid Staffordshire Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Mental Health Foundation of Mid Staffordshire National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Mental Health Foundation of Mid Staffordshire National Health Service Trust on 5th June 1992 trust property formerly held by Mid Staffordshire Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The South Devon Health Care National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
The Wirral Hospital National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Wirral Hospital National Health Service Trust on 5th June 1992 trust property formerly held by Wirral Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The A421 Road (M1 Motorway (Junction 13) to South Bedford) Order 1992
The Harwich Parkeston Quay Harbour Revision Order 1992
items.description: This Order empowers Sealink Harbours Limited to construct a new jetty and oil tanker berth, together with a walkway and pipetrack trestle bridge at Parkeston Quay and carry out such dredging operations as may be necessary in connection with these works. The order also extends the limits of jurisdiction of Sealink Harbours Limited at Parkeston Quay.

items.title: The Patents (Amendment) Rules 1992
The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No.3) Order 1992
The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1992
The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1992
The Education (Schools) Act 1992 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1992
The Tay River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Tweed River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Solway River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Forth River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The North East River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Highland River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Clyde River Purification Board (Establishment) Variation Order 1992
The Education (Further and Higher Education Institutions Access Funds) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The London Cab Order 1992
items.description: This Order increases the fares payable for the hiring of a motor cab in the Metropolitan Police District and the City of London in respect of all journeys beginning and ending there.

items.title: The Food Safety Act 1990 (Consequential Modifications) (Local Enactments) Order 1992
The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue West and Eastern Avenue East, Havering) (40 mph Speed Limit) Order 1992
The Building Regulations (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1991 (Commencement) Order 1992
The Academic Awards and Distinctions (Scotland) Order of Council 1992
items.description: This Order specifies Dundee Institute of Technology, Glasgow Polytechnic, Napier Polytechnic of Edinburgh, Paisley College of Technology and the Robert Gordon Institute of Technology as competent to grant degrees, diplomas, certificates or other academic awards or distinctions.

items.title: The National Health Service (District Health Authorities) Order 1992
The Gas Transit (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1992
The Artificial Insemination of Cattle (Animal Health) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1992
The A65 and A660 Trunk Roads (Burley-in-Wharfedale Bypass) Order 1992
The A65 and A660 Trunk Roads (Burley-in-Wharfedale) (Detrunking) Order 1992
The Company and Business Names (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Company and Business Names Regulations 1981 (as amended by the Company and Business Names (Amendment) Regulations 1982) (the  “1981 Regulations ”). The 1981 Regulations specify certain words and expressions for the purposes of section 26 of the Companies Act 1985 and section 2 of the Business Names Act 1985. Under those sections the Secretary of State’s approval is required for the use of such words and expressions in (respectively) a company or a business name.

items.title: The European Parliamentary (United Kingdom Representatives) Pensions (Amendment) Order 1992
The Income Support (General) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1992
The Income Tax (Deposit-takers) (Audit Powers) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 482(11) and 482A of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) and come into force on 30th January 1992. They provide firstly for the furnishing of information to, and inspection of documents by, the Board in connection with payments of interest made by deposit-takers without deduction of tax in respect of deposits which are not relevant deposits by reason of section 481(5) of the 1988 Act.

items.title: The National Health Service (Determination of Districts) Order 1992
items.description: This Order abolishes the districts of Cheltenham and District Health Authority and Gloucester Health Authority and merges the districts into a new district called Gloucestershire District.

items.title: The Horses (Protective Headgear for Young Riders) Act 1990 (Commencement) Order 1992
The Horses (Protective Headgear for Young Riders) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Horses (Protective Headgear for Young Riders) Act 1990. Section 1(1) of the 1990 Act makes it an offence for various persons (a parent, a guardian, an owner of a horse, a person who has custody of a horse immediately before a child rides it, an employer of a child) to cause or permit a child under the age of 14 to ride a horse (defined in section 3 of the 1990 Act to include a pony, mule, donkey or other equine animal) on a road unless the child is wearing protective headgear of such description as may be specified in the regulations and in such manner as may be so specified.

items.title: The Water (Timetable) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations prescribe dates, in relation to the financial year 1993-94 and subsequent financial years, by or before which certain matters relating principally to water supply functions in Scotland require to be done for the purposes of various enactments.

items.title: The Council Water Charge (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: Paragraph 11 of Schedule 11 to the Local Government Finance Act 1992 provides for the provisions of sections 71, 75 to 81, 96, 97 and 99(3) of that Act to have effect, subject to such adaptations, exceptions and modifications as may be prescribed, in relation to the council water charge as they have effect in relation to the council tax.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1992
The Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) (Wales) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only to schools in Wales, made under the Education Reform Act 1988 (“the Act”) revoke and re-enact with amendments, the provisions of the Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/1381). They require head teachers of schools maintained by local education authorities and of grant-maintained schools to provide information about individual pupils' educational achievements to parents of the pupils, including pupils over compulsory school age, together with information about the pupil’s attendance at school and the pupil’s general progress. Parents must also be given details of the arrangements for discussing a pupil’s report with his teachers.

items.title: The Private Legislation Procedure (Scotland) General Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the General Orders made under section 15 of the Private Legislation Procedure (Scotland) Act 1936, which regulate the procedure for obtaining Parliamentary powers by way of Provisional Order in matters affecting Scotland.

items.title: The Severn Bridges (Description of Vehicles) Order 1992
The Milk (Special Designation) Regulations (Amendment) Order 1992
The Road Traffic Offenders (Prescribed Devices) Order 1992
The A3 Trunk Road (Stoke Junction Improvement Guildford Detrunking) Order 1992
The Suckler Cow Premium (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend for the second time the Suckler Cow Premium Regulations 1991 ( “the principal Regulations”) and come into force on 15th June 1992.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 1992, supersede the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1991 (as amended by the Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) Regulations 1992), which are revoked (regulation 2).

items.title: The Undersized Whiting Order 1992
items.description: This Order prescribes a minimum size for the landing of whiting ( Merlangius merlangus) in Great Britain (article 2(1)). There is an exemption from the minimum landing size for the landing of whiting from foreign fishing boats (article 2(2)).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Carriage of Dangerous Goods and Substances) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend—

items.title: The Folkestone and District Water Company (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1992
items.description: Section 12(1) of the Statutory Water Companies Act 1991 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Temporary Restrictions) Procedure Regulations 1992
items.description: Sections 14, 15 and 16 of the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 enable a traffic authority (as defined in section 121A of the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984) to regulate traffic for temporary periods by order or notice. The New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 extends the power to issue such notices to concessionaires (as defined in that Act). New sections 14 and 15 are substituted and section 16 is amended by the Road Traffic (Temporary Restrictions) Act 1991 which comes into force on 1st July 1992.

items.title: Tramcars and Trolley Vehicles (Modification of Enactments) Regulations 1992
items.description: Section 141 of the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 as originally enacted provided that certain provisions of that Act did not apply to tramcars or trolley vehicles operated under statutory powers. Section 193 of the Road Traffic Act 1988 contained a similar provision. These sections are repealed by the Road Traffic Act 1991.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Temporary Restrictions) Act 1991 (Commencement) Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings the Road Traffic (Temporary Restrictions) Act 1991 into force on 1st July 1992. The Order contains transitional provisions.

items.title: The Land Registration (Open Register) Rules 1991
items.description: (This not does not form part of the Rules)

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Reserve Forces) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with the pension rights of pensionable employees under the Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1987 ( “the principal Regulations”) who are called out or volunteer for duty as reservists in the Armed Forces.

items.title: The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 (Commencement No.8) Order 1992
The Eastbourne Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Eastbourne Hospitals National Health Service Trust on 22nd June 1992 trust property formerly held by Eastbourne Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Gatwick Airport London (Cargo Area Designation) Order 1992
The Manchester International Airport (Cargo Area Designation) Order 1992
The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Amendment) Order 1992 (revoked)
items.description: This Order further amends the Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) Order 1986 (“the 1986 Order”) by substituting for the Schedule to that Order a new Schedule, in which the figures for quota per hectare prescribed by article 3 of that Order are revised (article 2). (The figures for the yield per hectare, prescribed by article 4 of the 1986 Order, remain unchanged.) This Order also amends article 2 of the 1986 Order to take account of the making of the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1992. Quota per hectare is prescribed for the purposes of calculating the payment which a tenant in England and Wales is entitled, in certain circumstances, to obtain from his landlord in respect of milk quota on the termination of the tenancy of the land, under Schedule 1 to the Agriculture Act 1986.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1992
The Legal Aid in Contempt of Court Proceedings (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the availability of legal aid to those against whom proceedings for contempt of court have been instituted.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Contempt of Court Proceedings (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide for the remuneration of solicitors and counsel giving legal aid in relation to contempt of court proceedings in Scotland. They provide for the fees and outlays allowable to solicitors (regulations 4 and 5); the fees and outlays allowable to Counsel (regulation 7); and for the submission of accounts by both (regulations 6 and 8). Taxation of fees and outlays is provided for in regulation 9.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 2) Order 1992
The Buying Agency Trading Fund (Variation) Order 1992
The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment (No. 2) Order 1992
items.description: This Order corrects two errors in the Table of Fees which the Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1992 substituted in the Court of Session etc. Fees Order 1984. Fees in respect of 2 categories of writs in proceedings in the Court of Session were increased in error from £40 to £73. This Order corrects that error.

items.title: The Tayside Regional Council (Backwater and Lintrathen Reservoirs) Byelaws Extension Order 1992
The Home-Grown Cereals Authority (Rate of Levy) Order 1992
The Lee Valley Water Plc (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1992
items.description: Section 12(1) of the Statutory Water Companies Act 1991 provides forthe replacement of provisions relating to the constitution andregulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts andOrders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which havebeen approved by special resolution of the members of the company. Thereplacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by theSecretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Rickmansworth Water Plc (Constitution and Regulation) Order1992
items.description: Section 12(1) of the Statutory Water Companies Act 1991 provides forthe replacement of provisions relating to the constitution andregulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts andOrders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which havebeen approved by special resolution of the members of the company. Thereplacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by theSecretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, Hillingdon) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1992
The Control of Gold, Securities, Payments and Credits (Serbia and Montenegro) Directions 1992
The Brechin and Bridge of Dun Light Railway Order 1992
The Manchester Ship Canal Harbour Revision Order 1992
The Revenue Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1992
items.description: This Order determines the amount of the revenue support grant payable to each local authority specified in Schedule 2 to the Order in respect of the financial year 1992-93.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1992, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1991 (as amended by the Education (Mandatory Awards) (Amendment) Regulations 1992) which are revoked (regulation 6(1)).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Cambridge City Council) Order 1992
The Export of Goods (Control) (Serbia and Montenegro Sanctions) Order 1992
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Dioxins) (England) (No. 2) Order 1992
The Statute Law (Repeals) Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1992
The Royal Cornwall Hospitals and West Cornwall Hospital National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1992
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS Trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Royal Cornwall Hospitals and West Cornwall Hospital National Health Service Trust to the Royal Cornwall Hospitals National Health Service Trust and makes consequential amendments to the Order.

items.title: The Police (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) so as to make provision for part-time service.

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 9 and Transitional Provision) Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 27th July 1992, section 13 of the Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (demolition of buildings) and section 32 of that Act (planning: minor and consequential amendments) so far as that section relates to paragraphs 10(2) and 52(2)(c) of Schedule 7 to the 1991 Act.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988.

items.title: The Walsall Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Walsall Hospitals National Health Service Trust on 30th June 1992 trust property formerly held by Walsall Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Free Zone (Port of Tilbury) Designation Order 1992
The Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks Deposits) Regulations 1992 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations apply to the making of deposits with the Accountant General of the Supreme Court under sections 144(1) and 146(2) of the Road Traffic Act 1988 ( “the Act”) in connection with provision against third-party risks arising out of the use of motor vehicles on roads, the investment of money or securities placed on deposit, and the withdrawal of such deposits. The Regulations revoke and replace the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks Deposits) Regulations 1967 and make the following main changes—

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Prohibition) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, amongst other things, replace with modifications the Goods Vehicles (Prohibitions) (Exemptions and Appeals) Regulations 1987 and the Public Passenger Vehicles (Exemptions, and Appeals Against Refusals to Issue Certificates or Remove Prohibitions) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Road Traffic Act 1991 (Commencement No 4 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1992
items.description: The Order brings into force on 1st July 1992 certain provisions of the Road Traffic Act 1991 including those relating to:—

items.title: The Firemen’s Pension Scheme Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings into operation the Firemen’s Pension Scheme 1992 set out in Schedule 2 to the Order (“the 1992 Scheme”) and revokes the Orders specified in Schedule 1. It effects the consolidation, with some further variations, of the Scheme brought into operation by the Firemen’s Pension Scheme Order 1973 as varied by the other Orders specified in Part I of Schedule 1. The further variations, apart from those that consist in the omission of spent or obsolete provisions, are described in the Table below.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Ministry of Defence Commercially Managed Ships) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the registration as British ships, for the purposes of the Merchant Shipping Acts 1894 to 1988 (the Acts), of a special class of Government ships, namely those in the service of the Ministry of Defence the commercial management of which the Secretary of State for Defence has entrusted to some other person pursuant to a contract.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Ministry of Defence Yachts) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the registration as British ships, for the purposes of the Merchant Shipping Acts 1894 to 1988 (the Acts), of a special class of Government ships, namely sailing yachts belonging to the Secretary of State for Defence and in the service of the Ministry of Defence.

items.title: The Education (Chief Inspector of Schools in England) Order 1992
The Transfer of Functions (Science) Order 1992
items.description: This Order in Council, made under the Ministers of the Crown Act 1975, transfers to the Minister for the Civil Service ( “the Minister”) the functions of the Secretary of State for Education and Science under sections 2 and 3 of, and Schedules 1 and 3 to, the Science and Technology Act 1965, under section 1(1) of the Agricultural Research etc. (Pensions) Act 1961 and under the Royal Charters of the five named Research Councils.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Small Businesses) Order 1992
The Arms Control and Disarmament (Privileges and Immunities) Act 1988 (Overseas Territories) Order 1992
items.description: This Order extends the provisions of section 1(2) to (4) of the Arms Control and Disarmament (Privileges and Immunities) Act 1988, subject to specified modifications, to the Territories listed in the Schedule.

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Deposit-takers) (Interest Payments) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under sections 480B and 482(11) (aa) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 and come into force on 30th January 1992, amend the Income Tax (Deposit-takers) (Interest Payments) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2232). In addition to making changes of a drafting nature, the amendments extend the categories of persons who can sign certificates of non-liability to tax to specified persons who may sign on behalf of persons suffering from mental disorder.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Days in Port) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the enforcement throughout the United Kingdom of Article 13(1) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3882/91 fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks, the total allowable catches for 1992 and certain conditions under which they may be fished (OJ No. L367, 31.12.91, p.1).

items.title: The Extradition (British Antarctic Territory) (Commonwealth Countries, Colonies and Republic of Ireland) Order 1992 (revoked)
items.description: This Order extends to the British Antarctic Territory, with certain exceptions, adaptations and modifications, the provisions of the Extradition Act 1989 relating to extradition between the United Kingdom and Commonwealth countries. It also applies those provisions as appropriate so as to regulate extradition between the Territory and the Republic of Ireland and between the Territory and the United Kingdom itself.

items.title: The Official Secrets Act 1989 (Hong Kong) Order 1992
items.description: This Order extends the Official Secrets Act 1989 to Hong Kong with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in Schedule 1 to this Order to form part of the law of Hong Kong. The exceptions, adaptations and modifications are required in order to reflect that Hong Kong is a dependent territory which has laws of its own, its own administration and permanent residents. The text of the Act, as extended to Hong Kong, is set out in full in Schedule 2 to the Order.

items.title: The Serbia and Montenegro (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 757 of 30th May 1992, on the export of goods to Serbia or Montenegro and on the supply of goods to persons connected therewith, and on the importation of goods therefrom. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships and aircraft. The Order places restrictions on the servicing and insuring of aircraft of Serbia and Montenegro. It also restricts the making of payments under any bond in respect of a contract the performance of which is unlawful by virtue of the Order, and prohibits the making or obtaining payments under certain indemnities in respect of such bonds. The Order also places restrictions on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds or other financial or economic resources to or for the benefit of persons connected with Serbia or Montenegro.

items.title: The Serbia and Montenegro (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 2. It imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 757 of 30th May 1992, on the export of goods to Serbia or Montenegro and on the supply of goods to persons connected therewith, and on the importation of goods therefrom. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships and aircraft. The Order places restrictions on the servicing and insuring of aircraft of Serbia and Montenegro. It also restricts the making of payments under any bond in respect of a contract the performance of which is unlawful by virtue of the Order, and prohibits the making or obtaining payments under certain indemnities in respect of such bonds. The Order also places restrictions on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds or other financial or economic resources to or for the benefit of persons connected with Serbia or Montenegro.

items.title: The Serbia and Montenegro (United Nations Prohibition of Flights) Order 1992
The Serbia and Montenegro (United Nations Prohibition of Flights) (Dependent Territories) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, implements a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 757 of 30th May 1992 by prohibiting aircraft from taking off from, landing in or overflying the territories specified in the Schedule, if they are destined for, or have taken off from, Serbia or Montenegro.

items.title: The Copyright (Isle of Man) (Revocation) Order 1992
The Serbia and Montenegro (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes in the Channel Islands restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No.757 of 30th May 1992, on the export of goods to Serbia and Montenegro and on the supply of goods to persons connected therewith, and on the importation of goods therefrom. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships and aircraft. The Order places restrictions on the servicing and insurance of aircraft of Serbia and Montenegro. It also restricts the making of payments under any bond in respect of a contract the performance of which is unlawful by virtue of the Order and prohibits the making of or obtaining payments under certain indemnities in respect of such bonds. The Order also places restrictions on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds or other financial or economic resources to or for the benefit of persons connected with Serbia or Montenegro.

items.title: The Chester and Halton Community National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Chester and Halton Community National Health Service Trust on 24th February 1992 trust property formerly held by Halton Health Authority. A copy of the schedules of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House,79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (National Heritage) Order 1992
items.description: This Order constitutes the Secretary of State for National Heritage a corporation sole with a corporate seal.

items.title: The Social Security (Australia) Order 1992 (revoked)
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Act 1975 and Part I of the Child Benefit Act 1975 so as to give effect to the Agreement on Social Security (set out in Schedule 1), as modified by the provisions contained in Schedule 3 to the Order, made between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of Australia. The Agreement relates to reciprocity in contributions, old age pensions, widow’s benefit, family allowance, guardian’s allowance, sickness benefit, invalidity benefit and unemployment benefit.

items.title: The Copyright (Application to the Isle of Man) Order 1992
The Transfers of Functions (Energy) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Secretary of State for Trade and Industry all the functions vested under various enactments in the Secretary of State for Energy.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Financial Services) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers a number of functions currently exercisable by the Secretary of State to the Treasury with effect from 7th June, so as to be exercisable by the Treasury alone, by the Secretary of State and the Treasury acting jointly or by the Secretary of State and the Treasury acting concurrently. It also makes a number of ancillary and supplementary provisions.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Inner City Task Forces) Order 1992
The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1992
The Epsom Health Care National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Epsom Health Care National Health Service Trust on trust property formerly held by the Mid Surrey Health Authority. A copy of the schedules of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House,79 Whitehall, London SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Friendly Societies Act 1992 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1992
The Income-Related Benefits Amendment Regulations 1992
The Devon Ambulance Service National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1992
The Housing (Disapplication of Financial Hardship Provision for Repairs Grant) (Scotland) Order 1992
The Council Tax (Valuation of Dwellings) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations relate to properties in Scotland which are dwellings for council tax purposes. Section 72(2) of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 ( “the 1992 Act”) defines the properties which are dwellings for those purposes.

items.title: The North Middlesex Hospital National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the North Middlesex Hospital National Health Service Trust on24th February 1992 trust property formerly held by the Haringey Health Authority. A copy of the schedules of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Richmond House,79 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Council Tax (Contents of Valuation Lists) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: Section 84(2) of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 provides that a council tax valuation list in Scotland must show, for each day for which it is in force, each dwelling in the regional or islands council’s area and which of the valuation bands is applicable to the dwelling. These Regulations prescribe that the list must, in addition, show the reference number ascribed to each dwelling, a note of the day from which any alterations to the list have effect, (if applicable) a note that an alteration was made pursuant to an order of a valuation appeal committee or the Court of Session, and notes indicating which dwellings are private garages or private storage premises.

items.title: The Council Tax (Liability of Owners) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 provides that the council tax in respect of dwellings in Scotland shall come into operation with effect from financial year 1993/94.

items.title: The Council Tax (Administration and Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the administration and enforcement of council tax and the council water charge in Scotland under the Local Government Finance Act 1992 and for related matters.

items.title: The Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) (Scotland) Order 1992
items.description: This Order prescribes classes of dwelling in Scotland in respect of which there will be no liability for council tax or the council water charge. The Schedule to the Order lists the classes in question.

items.title: The Council Tax (Dwellings) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide that certain private garages, carports and car parking stances and certain private storage premises are to constitute “dwellings” for the purposes of council tax in Scotland.

items.title: The Council Tax (Reductions for Disabilities) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: Section 78 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 ( “the Act”) provides for the calculation of the basic amounts which persons are liable to pay by way of council tax in respect of dwellings in Scotland. By virtue of paragraph 11 of Schedule 11 to the Act, section 78 also provides similarly in respect of the council water charge.

items.title: The Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act 1992 (Commencement) Order 1992
The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1992
The Royal London Hospital and Associated Community Services National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1992
items.description: This Order transfers to the Royal London Hospital and Associated Community Services National Health Service Trust on 24th February 1992 trust property formerly held by Tower Hamlets Health Authority. A copy of the schedules of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, National Health Trust Unit, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London SW1A 2NS.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1992
The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 so as to modify the calculation of pensions in cases where there has been part-time service (introduced by concurrent amendments to the Police Regulations 1987).

items.title: The Sea Fish (Specified Sea Areas) (Regulation of Nets and Other Fishing Gear) (Variation) Order 1992
The Civil Courts (Amendment No. 2) Order 1992
The Finance Act 1991, section 58, (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 1992
items.description: Section 58 of the Finance Act 1991 makes further provision with regard to manufactured dividends and interest, and has effect in relation to payments made on or after such day as the Treasury may specify. Different days may be appointed for different provisions or different purposes. Subsection (1) of section 58 makes provision for section 736A to be inserted into the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988, and subsection (2) of section 58 introduces Schedule 13 to the 1991 Act. Paragraph 1 of Schedule 13 inserts Schedule 23A into the 1988 Act, paragraph 2 of Schedule 13 makes an amendment to Schedule 18 to the Finance Act 1986 (c. 41), and paragraphs 3 and 4 of Schedule 13 amend sections 737 and 738 of the 1988 Act respectively.

items.title: The Transport and Works Act 1992 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th July 1992 the provisions of the Transport and Works Act 1992 ( “the Act”) specified in the Schedule to the Order. These include certain provisions relating to the safety of railways and other forms of transport, empowering the Secretary of State to give directions in relation to speeds, loads and insurance cover and making improved provision for the securing of railway crossing gates and barriers. A power of leasing is conferred on certain tramway undertakers.

items.title: The Education Assets Board (Transfers under the Education Reform Act 1988) Regulations 1992
The Neath Canal (Cnel Bach) (Repeal of Local Enactments) Order 1992
items.description: This Order extinguishes navigation rights over the Cnel Bach stretch of the Neath Canal.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Cargo Ship Construction and Survey) Regulations 1984 (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which amend the Merchant Shipping (Cargo Ship Construction and Survey) Regulations 1984, give effect to the amendments to Chapter II-I of the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea, 1974 (SOLAS) which were adopted by the Maritime Safety Committee of the International Maritime Organisation on 25th May 1990 by Resolution MSC 19(58).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Approved Investments) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Approved Investments) Regulations 1990 which contain a list of investments approved by the Secretary of State for the purposes of Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (revenue accounts and capital finance of local authorities).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Large Goods and Passenger-Carrying Vehicles) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1992
The Food (Lot Marking) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, come into force on 1st July 1992. They implement Council Directive 89/396/EEC (OJ No. L186, 30.6.89, p. 21), as amended by Council Directives 91/238/EEC (OJ No. L107, 27.4.91, p. 50) and 92/11/EEC (OJ No. L65, 11.3.92, p. 32), on indications and marks identifying the lot to which a foodstuff belongs. Subject to exceptions the Regulations require food which has been produced, prepared or packaged as part of a lot to be so marked or labelled as to enable the lot to be identified (regulation 3).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains) Designation (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains) Designation Order 1986 which designated an area of the Cambrian Mountains as an environmentally sensitive area.

items.title: The Competition and Service (Utilities) Act 1992 Designation Order 1992
items.description: This Order provides that the designated operators for the purposes of sections 27A to 27K of the Telecommunications Act 1984 (which were inserted by the Competition and Service (Utilities) Act 1992) shall be British Telecommunications plc and Kingston Communications (Hull) PLC.

items.title: The Sheep Scab (Revocation) Order 1992
items.description: This Order revokes the Sheep Scab (National Dip) Order 1990 as amended and the Sheep Scab Order 1992. It makes consequential amendments to the Transit of Animals Order 1927, the Animals (Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 1927 and the Importation of Animals Order 1977.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
Motorways Traffic (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motorways Traffic (England and Wales) Regulations 1982.

items.title: The Army Terms of Service Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, together with the Army Terms of Service (Part-time Service in Northern Ireland) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1366), re-enact with amendments the Army Terms of Service Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/2072) as amended (the 1986 Regulations). Apart from minor and drafting amendments, these Regulations make the following changes of substance in respect of enlistment in the regular army other than for part-time service in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Army Terms of Service (Part-time Service in Northern Ireland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide for terms and conditions of service of persons enlisted in the regular army for part-time service in Northern Ireland. Together with the Army Terms of Service Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1365) they replace the Army Terms of Service Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/2072) as amended. They make provisions as to call-out similar to those in the Reserve Forces Act 1980 (c. 9) (the 1980 Act) which applied to the Ulster Defence Regiment before the coming into force of the Army Act 1992 (c. 39).

items.title: The Control of Pollution (Licensing of Waste Disposal) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Pollution (Licensing of Waste Disposal) (Scotland) Regulations 1977 which provide for a number of matters concerning the licensing of sites for the disposal of controlled waste under Part I of the Control of Pollution Act 1974.

items.title: The Blood Tests (Evidence of Paternity) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Motor Vehicles (Off Road Events) Regulations 1992
items.description: As a result of amendments made to the Road Traffic Act 1988 by the Road Traffic Act 1991 coming into force on 1st July 1992 offences under sections 1, 2 or 3 of the 1988 Act will be capable of being committed in public places which are not roads. The offences under sections 1, 2 and 3 (as amended) will be causing death by dangerous driving, dangerous driving, and careless and inconsiderate driving.

items.title: The Special Roads (Notice of Opening) Regulations 1992
The London Priority Route Order 1992
The A1 Trunk Road (Holloway Road, Islington) Experimental Traffic Order 1992
The County Council of Norfolk (A1151 Wroxham/Hoveton Bypass, River Bure Bridge) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1992
The Sheriffs' Fees (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order alters the fees payable to sheriffs for executing writs of possession of property in four substantive ways. First, it alters the basis for calculating fees in respect of domestic property to take account of the abolition of domestic rating by the Local Government Finance Act 1988. Where a value for the whole or any part of the property is ascertainable from the valuation list in force immediately before 1 April 1990, when the abolition occurred, and the property was domestic property at that time, that value will form the basis for calculating the fee in respect of the property or the relevant part of it. Otherwise, the fee will be calculated by reference to the value by the year of the property or the relevant part of it.

items.title: The Portsmouth (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1992
The Control of Gold, Securities, Payments and Credits (Serbia and Montenegro) (Revocation) Directions 1992
The Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, made under section 8 of the Education Act 1980, section 22 of the Education Reform Act 1988 and section 16 of the Education (Schools) Act 1992, apply mainly to secondary schools maintained by local education authorities and to grant-maintained schools in England. They replace the Education (Information on School Examination Results) (England) 1991, which are revoked.

items.title: The Sea Fish Industry Authority (Levy) (Amendment) Regulations 1992 Confirmatory Order 1992
items.description: This Order confirms Regulations made by the Sea Fish Industry Authority which amend the Sea Fish Industry Authority (Levy) Regulations 1988. The amending Regulations reduce most of the rates of the levy imposed by that Authority in respect of sea fish and sea fish products for the purpose of financing its activities. The old rates of levy are shown in italics in the revised Schedule to the 1988 Regulations.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) Order 1992
The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1991 (Continuance) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Deposit-takers) (Non-residents) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 30th January 1992, are made under section 482(11) and (12) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”). They provide for the giving of a certificate by a deposit-taker where a declaration made to the deposit-taker under section 481(5) (k) of the 1988 Act, that the person beneficially entitled to interest in respect of a deposit is not ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom, does not give the address of the person making it. The making of a declaration in respect of a deposit, supported by a certificate where necessary, enables a deposit-taker to make payments of interest in respect of that deposit without deduction of income tax.

items.title: The Council Tax (Discounts) (Scotland) Order 1992
items.description: Under Part II of, and Schedule 11 to, the Local Government Act 1992 “the Act”), the council tax and the council water charge become payable in Scotland with effect from 1st April 1993. The amounts payable may be reduced where a person resident in a dwelling falls to be disregarded for the purposes of discount. Schedule 1 to the Act provides for classes of persons to qualify for the purposes of discount. This Order makes further provisions in relation to certain of those classes.

items.title: The Council Tax (Discounts) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: Part II of, and Schedule 11 to, the Local Government Finance Act 1992 ( “the Act”) provide that the council tax and the council water charge in respect of dwellings in Scotland shall come into operation with effect from financial year 1993/94. The amounts payable may be reduced where a person resident in a dwelling qualifies to be disregarded for the purposes of discount. Schedule 1 to the Act specifies classes of person who qualify to be disregarded for the purposes of discount. These Regulations make additional provision in this regard as respects Scotland.

items.title: The Road Traffic Act 1991 (Commencement No. 4 and Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic Act 1991 (Commencement No. 4 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1992 so as to correct a textual error.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency and Prevention of Terrorism Provisions) (Continuance) Order 1992
