items.title: The Planning (Hazardous Substances) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Planning (Hazardous Substances) Act 1990. That Act provides that the presence of or above the controlled quantity of a hazardous substance on, over or under land, requires hazardous substances consent.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1992
The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988.

items.title: The National Health Service Functions (Administration Arrangements and Amendment of Directions) Regulations 1992
The National Health Service (Appellate and Other Functions) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide for the exercise by the Yorkshire Regional Health Authority of certain appellate and other functions conferred on the Secretary of State, under provisions of Regulations listed in the Schedule, in connection with decisions and functions of Family Health Services Authorities (regulation 3). Provision is also made requiring the Yorkshire Regional Health Authority to make arrangements for the functions conferred on it under these Regulations to be discharged on its behalf either by an officer of the Authority or by a committee established in accordance with the Regulations, according to the nature of the function in question (regulation 4).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide for the arrangements under which general dental services are provided under Part II of the National Health Service Act 1977. They supersede the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1973 (“the 1973 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, those provisions of The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations) which relate to pharmaceutical services. The provisions of the 1974 Regulations which relate to General Medical Services are consolidated, with amendments, in The National Health Service (General Medical Services) Regulations 1992. The 1974 Regulations and all subsequent amendments to them are revoked by regulation 26 and Schedule 4. These Regulations now regulate the terms on which pharmaceutical services are provided under The National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Attachment of Earnings Order) (Wales) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations prescribe words in Welsh and English for use by Welsh charging authorities in attachment of earnings orders in place of part of the text of the attachment of earnings order prescribed in the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/438), as amended by the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/219).

items.title: The National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations supersede the National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations”) to provide for the investigation and determination by Family Health Services Authorities (“FHSAs”) and their committees, of questions whether doctors, dentists, chemists and opticians providing services under Part II of the National Health Service Act 1977 have failed to comply with their terms of service, and for the consideration and determination of related questions. The Regulations also provide for the determination of representations and applications to the National Health Service Tribunal (“the Tribunal”), and for appeals from determinations of FHSAs and the Tribunal.

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 8) Order 1992
The Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace with amendments the Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations 1989 and two amending instruments.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1992
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 5) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Days in Port) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Sea Fishing (Days in Port) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision for the enforcement throughout the United Kingdom of Article 13(1) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3882/91 (“the Council Regulation”) fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks the total allowable catches for 1992 and certain conditions under which they may be fished.

items.title: The Artificial Insemination of Cattle (Animal Health) (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Artificial Insemination of Cattle (Animal Health) (England and Wales) Regulations 1985 as follows—

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom and come into force on 2nd April 1992, provide for the enforcement of EEC Regulations (as specified in Schedules 1 and 2) concerned with the production and marketing of wine and related products. They revoke and re-enact with amendments the provisions of the Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) Regulations 1990 (S.I.1990/1363).

items.title: The A38, A40 and A417 Trunk Roads (Gloucester City) (Detrunking) Order 1992
The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1992

The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) (Bus Lanes) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1992

The Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Animal Use) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Animal Use) Regulations 1991, as amended, prescribe fees in connection with applications and inspections relating to licences and certificates granted under the Medicines Act 1968 insofar as they apply to medicinal products for animal use. Most fees are increased by approximately 5%.

items.title: The Oilseeds Producers (Support System) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 3rd April and apply in Great Britain, supplement Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3766/91 establishing a support system for producers of soya beans, rape seed and colza seed and sunflower seed (OJ No. L356, 24.12.91, p.17) (“Council Regulation”) and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 615/92 laying down detailed rules for a support system for producers of soya beans, rape seed, colza seed and sunflower seed (OJ No. L67, 12.3.92, p.11) (“Commission Regulation”).

items.title: The Veterinary Surgery (Epidural Anaesthesia) Order 1992
items.description: This Order permits the administration of epidural anaesthesia for the purpose of bovine embryo collection or transfer by persons not qualified as veterinary surgeons or practitioners, which would otherwise be prohibited by section 19(1) of the Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966.

items.title: The Levying Bodies (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Valuation for Rating (Former Enterprise Zones) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1992
The Education (School Information) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1992
The National Assistance (Charges for Accommodation) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations replace, with effect from 6th April 1992, the National Assistance (Charges for Accommodation) (Scotland) Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Community Charge Benefit (Subsidy) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations provide for the particulars, which are required to be provided by an authority to the Secretary of State on the making of claims for housing benefit subsidy and community charge benefit subsidy, to include also details of rent rebate expenditure attributable to accommodation for homeless persons and certain other short term accommodation and of instruments of payment which have not been encashed.

items.title: The Personal Injuries (Civilians) Amendment Scheme 1992
The Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991 by–

items.title: The Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: Regulation 2 amends regulation 3 of the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Regulations 1984 by changing the conditions for residence and presence in Great Britain that a person must satisfy to be entitled to severe disablement allowance. A person must be ordinarily resident and present in Great Britain and have been present for at least 26 weeks in the preceding year. Additional presence conditions apply to persons in receipt of tax free emoluments.

items.title: The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”) in consequence of the introduction as social security benefits, by the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991, of disability living allowance and disability working allowance and of the replacement by disability living allowance of mobility allowance and (for people disabled before the age of 65) attendance allowance.

items.title: The Representation of the People (Variation of Limits of Candidates' Election Expenses) Order 1992
items.description: This Order increases the maximum amounts of candidates' election expenses at parliamentary elections in the United Kingdom (articles 2 to 5), local government elections in Great Britain (article 6) and ward elections (article 7) and elections by liverymen in common hall (article 8) in the City of London.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) Order 1992 (revoked)
The Football Spectators (Corresponding Offences in Sweden) Order 1992
items.description: Article 2 of, and Schedule 1 to, this Order specify offences under the law of Sweden which appear to Her Majesty to correspond to certain of the offences specified in Schedule 1 to the Football Spectators Act 1989. Under section 22 of that Act proceedings may be commenced before magistrates against a person who resides or is believed to reside in an area in England or Wales if that person has been convicted of such an offence. Under such proceedings a restriction order may be made against such a person. Under section 19 of that Act the person to whom such an order applies may be required to report to a police station in England or Wales on the occasion of a football match played in any country outside England and Wales of a description for the time being designated by order under section 14(2) of that Act.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Magistrates' Courts and Family Law) Order 1992
The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 6) (Scotland) Order 1992
The Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment Order 1992
items.description: This Order further amends the Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for pensions and other awards in respect of disablement or death due to service in the naval, military and air forces during the 1914 World War and after 2nd September 1939.

items.title: The Gas Appliances (Safety) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive No. 90/396/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the member States relating to appliances burning gaseous fuels (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Telecommunication Meters (Approval Fees) (British Approvals Board for Telecommunications) Order 1992
items.description: This Order provides for the charging of fees in respect of the approval of meters under section 24 of the Telecommunications Act 1984.

items.title: The Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) Regulations 1986 to increase the fees for the approval of fitters or workshops for the installation or repair of recording equipment (Tachographs). These fees were last increased in April 1991.

items.title: The International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (Fees) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) as follows.

The Financial Markets and Insolvency (Amendment) Regulations 1992

items.description: These Regulations amend regulations 64 of the Representation of the People Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) to take account of changes made by the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). items.description: This Order brings into force the Planning (Hazardous Substances) Act 1990.

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1991 (Suspension of Prisoner Custody Officer Certificate) Regulations 1992
items.description: Paragraph 3 of Schedule 10 to the Criminal Justice Act 1991 provides that if it appears to a prisoner escort monitor or (as the case may be) controller that a prisoner custody officer is not a fit and proper person to perform escort duties or (as the case may be) custodial duties he may refer the matter to the Secretary of State to decide whether to revoke that officer’s certificate and may, in prescribed circumstances, suspend the officer’s certificate pending the Secretary of State’s decision.

items.title: The Social Security (Introduction of Disability Living Allowance) Miscellaneous Amendments Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Introduction of Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 (“the Introduction Regulations”) and the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance (Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1991 (“the Consequential Regulations”).

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Miscellaneous Amendments) Rules 1992
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts Rules 1981 in four respects.

The Returning Officer’s Charges (Northern Ireland) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, which applies in Northern Ireland, revokes and replaces the Returning Officer’s Charges (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 (“the 1991 Order”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Cash Equivalents of Car Benefits) Order 1992
The Income Tax (Cash Equivalents of Car Fuel Benefits) Order 1992
The Value Added Tax (Increase of Consideration for Fuel) Order 1992
The Vocational Training (Public Financial Assistance and Disentitlement to Tax Relief) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations, made under section 32 of the Finance Act 1991, (“section 32”) contain provisions supplementary to those in section 32 which make provision for tax relief for payments in respect of qualifying courses of vocational training to be given by deduction from such payments of amounts equal to income tax at the basic rate on them (“relief at source”). They specify the types of public financial assistance that will preclude the giving of relief under section 32(2). They also make provision for an individual making a payment in respect of a course of vocational training to have his entitlement to tax relief on that payment removed in certain circumstances, for the circumstances in which tax for which relief has been given at source may be recovered from the training provider to whom the payment was made, and for the adaptation of section 30 of the Taxes Management Act 1970 in order to secure that recovery by way of assessment.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Pensions Increase (Judicial Pensions) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pensions Increase (Judicial Pensions) Regulation 1972 (“the 1972 Regulations”), as amended in particular by the Pensions Increase (Judicial Pensions) (Amendment) Regulations 1978, and the Pensions Increase (Judicial Pensions) (Amendment) Regulations 1991 (“the 1991 Regulations”). Regulation 7(3) of the 1972 Regulations provided, before its effect was amended by the 1991 Regulations, that, for the purposes of the Pensions (Increase) Act 1971, in calculating the increase in a pension to which the Regulation applied, it was deemed that the pension had begun on the date when the pension payable to a person resigning the office of Lord Chancellor was last increased. The 1991 Regulations amended the effect of regulation 7(3) so that it did not apply to the calculation of any increase in a pension payable to or in respect of a person who ceases to hold the office of Lord Chancellor on or after 12 April 1991, except in the case of the person who held the office of Lord Chancellor on 28 February 1991. The amendment in regulation 2 of these Regulations makes it clear that in so far as regulation 7(3) of the 1972 Regulations still applies, it applies either to a pension payable under section 3 of the Lord Chancellor’s Pension Act 1832 or to a pension payable under Part IIof the Judicial Pensions Act 1981 for a widow or children of a deceased Lord Chancellor.

items.title: The Price Indications (Bureaux de Change) (No.2) Regulations 1992 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Price Indications (Bureaux de Change) Regulations 1992 before those Regulations come into force.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Community Charge Benefit (Subsidy) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the calculation of housing benefit and community charge benefit subsidy payable under the Social Security Act 1986 to authorities administering housing benefit and community charge benefit.

The Building (Prescribed Fees etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1992

The Civil Jurisdiction and Judgments Act 1991 (Commencement) Order 1992
The Vocational Training (Tax Relief) Regulations 1992

The Regulations also provide the machinery by which the training providers receiving the payments from which amounts have been deducted can recover those amounts from the Board by making claims; and provide for the inspection of records kept by training providers and for training providers to furnish information.

items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Table of Fees in the Schedule to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) 1989 by increasing fees payable to solicitors by about 8 per cent.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978, and for payment of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital. The time limit for making claims for repayment is extended from one month to three months (regulation 2). The extended time limit applies only in respect of charges paid or travelling expenses incurred on or after 6th April 1992 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Medicines (Applications for Grant and Renewal of Licences) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Medicines (Applications for Product Licences and Clinical Trial Certificates and Animal Test Certificates) Regulations 1971 and the Medicines (Renewal Applications for Licences and Certificates) Regulations 1974 by adding to the requirements as to particulars to be contained in, or to accompany, an application for the grant or renewal of a product licence, so implementing in part Council Directives—

items.title: The Medicines (Products for Human Use– Fees) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Products for Human Use– Fees) Regulations 1991 which prescribe fees in connection with applications and inspections relating to licences and certificates granted under the Medicines Act 1968 and also prescribe periodic fees in connection with the holding of such licences, insofar as they apply to medicinal products for human use only.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Art) (Wales) Order 1992
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Music) (Wales) Order 1992
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

The Weights and Measures Act 1985 (Commencement) Order 1992

items.description: This Order prohibits the fishing for or taking of freshwater fish in the area prescribed, without legal right or written permission from someone having that right.

items.title: The Highlands and Islands Rural Enterprise Programme (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Highlands and Islands Rural Enterprise Programme Regulations 1991.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (Amendment) 1992
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends certain fees in the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1990 (S.I. 1990/381).

items.title: The New Towns (Defects Grants) (Payments to District Councils) (Extensions of Time) (Amendment) Order 1992
The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 1992
The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Amendment No. 7) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, which comes into force forthwith upon being made, amends the Customs Duties (ECSC) Order 1987 which, with exceptions for certain countries, charges customs duties in accordance with the unified European Coal and Steel Community (ECSC) tariff on certain ECSC products imported into the United Kingdom.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1992
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules 1981 so as—

items.title: The County Court (Forms)(Amendment) Rules 1992
The Social Security (Class 1 Contributions – Contracted-out Percentages) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by a resolution of each House of Parliament, is made consequent upon the third review made under section 28 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”), following a report by the Government Actuary under that section in relation to the contracted-out percentages under section 27 of the 1975 Act and to changes in the cost to occupational pension schemes of providing guaranteed minimum pensions. The Order decreases, with effect from 6th April 1993, the contracted-out percentages to be deducted from the normal percentage for both primary and secondary Class 1 contributions. Consequential changes are also made to paragraph 2(2) of Schedule 3 to the 1975 Act, (priority in bankruptcy: calculation of employer’s contribution to an occupational pension scheme), by article 3 of this Order.

items.title: The State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations revoke and supersede the State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Regulations 1987 and the State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Amendment Regulations 1988 and 1991 (which amended the Regulations of 1987). They prescribe the tables in accordance with which the Secretary of State is required to make calculations in relation to, and for the purpose of determining the amount of, state scheme premiums (other than contributions equivalent premiums).

items.title: The Statutory Sick Pay (Small Employers' Relief) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the Statutory Sick Pay (Small Employers' Relief) Regulations 1991 (“the 1991 Regulations”) by—1amending the meaning of “small employer” so that an employer is a small employer when his contributions payments for the qualifying tax year (defined in the 1991 Regulations) did not exceed £16,000 rather than £15,000 as previously, and2making provision for calculating the entitlement threshold (above which a small employer qualifies for small employers' relief in respect of the statutory sick pay he pays to an employee) when there is more than one weekly rate of statutory sick pay in an employee’s period of incapacity for work.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Coal Mining Subsidence Act 1991) 1992
items.description: Sections 41 and 42 of the Coal Mining Subsidence Act 1991 provide for disputes about the withholding of agreement or consent and about access to premises to be resolved by application to the sheriff. This Act of Sederunt provides that such an application is to be made by way of summary application.

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Com mencement No.3 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 1990 to postpone from 31st March 1992 to 30th June 1992 the coming into force of section 27(1) and (2) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 insofar as these provisions concern a State Hospital Management Committee within the meaning of the Mental Health (Scotland) Act 1984.

items.title: The British Technology Group Act 1991 (Modification and Saving) Order 1992
The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Foresterhill Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends articles 1 and 2 of the Foresterhill Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991 to change the name of the Trust to “The Aberdeen Royal Hospitals National Health Service Trust”. It also amends article 4 of that Order to make clear that the number of executive directors of the Trust shall be five.

items.title: The South Ayrshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends article 4 of the South Ayrshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991 to make clear that the number of executive directors of the Trust shall be five.

items.description: This Order amends the Civil Aviation Act 1982 (Guernsey) Order 1992 (“the principal Order”). The effect of the amendments in paragraphs (2) and (4) of article 2 is to revoke the extension to the Bailiwick of Guernsey of section 88 of the Civil Aviation Act 1982 (detention and sale of aircraft for unpaid airport charges) as modified by the principal Order; that section is excluded from the power contained in section 108(1) of that Act of 1982, under which the principal Order was made. The amendment in paragraph (3) of article 2 is consequential.

items.title: The Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland Consequential Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes an amendment to the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 which is consequential on changes in the law relating to the definition and status of trade unions and employers' associations in Northern Ireland contained in Part II of the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1992.

items.title: The Local Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends paragraph 6 of Part I of Schedule 2 to the Local Elections (Northern Ireland) Order 1985, as substituted by S.I. 1987/168, (“the 1985 Order”) to take account of changes made by the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 (“the 1991 Order”). The 1985 Order makes provision for local elections in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The National Health Service (Indicative Amounts) (Scotland) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the calculation of the basic price of drugs, medicines and listed appliances for the purposes of enabling Health Boards to specify indicative amounts to doctors' practices in terms of their duty under section 87D of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (c. 29).

items.title: The Social Security (Barbados) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Act 1975 and that part of the Child Benefit Act 1975 which makes provision for Child Benefit, so as to give effect to the Convention on social security and Protocol which were signed on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of Barbados on 7th January 1992.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Investment Advertisements) (Exemptions) (No. 2) Order 1992
items.description: The order provides various exemptions from the restrictions on advertising imposed by section 57 of the Financial Services Act 1986. Article 2 of the order concerns advertisements directed at informing or influencing persons of the kind described in the article. Articles 3 and 4 give certain indications as to whether an advertisement may be regarded as being directed at informing or influencing persons of the relevant kind. Article 5 of the order concerns advertisements relating to an investment falling within any of paragraphs 1 to 5 of Schedule 1 to the Financial Services Act 1986 which is traded or dealt in on a market of a kind referred to in the article. Article 6 of the order concerns advertisements issued or caused to be issued by a market of a kind referred to in the article which identify a particular investment falling within paragraph 7, 8 or 9 of Schedule 1 to the Financial Services Act as one which can be traded or dealt in on the market.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1992
The Land Registration (Scotland) Act 1979 (Commencement No.5) Order 1992
The Commonwealth Development Corporation (Raising of Limits on Borrowing and Advances) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, which is made under sections 9A(2) and 10(1) of the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1978, increases the limit on borrowing etc by the Commonwealth Development Corporation from £750 million to £850 million, and the limit on advances made by the Secretary of State to the Corporation from £700 million to £800 million. The sums required by the Secretary of State for the purposes of such advances to the Corporation are paid out of money provided by Parliament.

items.title: The Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 (Commencement No.1 and Saving Provisions) Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings into force on 25th April 1992 section 44 of the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 (for a limited purpose only) and on 16th May, 1st June, 1st September 1992 and 1st April 1993 the provisions of that Act specified respectively in Sched ules 1, 2, 3 and 4 to the Order.

items.title: The Local Government Finance Act 1992 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1992
items.description: This Order provides for the commencement on 1st April 1992 of certain provisions of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the Act”) relating to Scotland.

items.title: The Licensing (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 1992 (Commencement and Savings) Order 1992
items.description: This Order commences, on 15th April 1992, section 1 of the Licensing (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 1992. That section amends section 25 of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 to enable licensing boards to transfer liquor licences by a one stage procedure, in addition to the existing two stage procedure; and also amends section 19 of the 1976 Act which makes it a criminal offence in certain circumstances to canvas licensing board members.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1992
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1990 by increasing the fees payable to Sheriff Officers by about 6%. The last increase was in the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1991 (S.I. 1991/290). It also amends the entry in the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1990 (S.I. 1990/379) relating to photocopies.

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Fees) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations replace the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Fees) Regulations 1991 (“the previous Regulations ”) which are revoked by S.I. 1992/56). They are made in consequence of the functions of the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food relating to the type approval of agricultural and forestry tractors and their components becoming functions of the Secretary of State by virtue of the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/80).

items.title: The Further and Higher Education Act 1992 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1992
items.description: This Order brings into force the provisions of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 specified in the Schedules thereto on the dates specified in the Schedules.

items.title: The Representation of the People (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 64 of the Representation of the People (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) to take account of changes made by the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 (“the 1991 Order”). The 1986 Regulations apply in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend the European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1986 which provide for the conduct of European Parliamentary elections in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Representation of the People (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 62 of the Representation of the People (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) to take account of changes made by the Disability Living Allowance and the Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”).

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Oxfordshire) Order 1992
The Petty Sessional Divisions (Essex) Order 1992
The Combined Probation Areas (Oxfordshire) Order 1992
The Combined Probation Areas (Essex) Order 1992
The Magistrates' Courts Fees (Amendment) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes new provision for court fees relating to civil business in magistrates' courts. It considerably shortens the current list in Part I of Schedule 6 to the Magistrates' Courts Act 1980 and, among other changes, introduces new fees in relation to family proceedings and the collection of the community charge and rates. It also increases significantly the fees for those items which have been retained in the list, in order to reflect more accurately the cost of the services involved. It does not apply to legally assisted persons, or persons in receipt of family credit or income support.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Dioxins) (England) Order 1992
items.description: This Order prohibits the breeding of food creatures and the movement of such creatures and crops from the designated area (articles 3 and 4 and the Schedule). The creatures, crops and their produce may create a hazard to human health because they may be contaminated with dioxins (article 2).

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue East and Eastern Avenue West, Havering) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1992
The A23 Trunk Road (Foxley Lane, Croydon) (Prohibition of Right Turn) Order 1992
The Industrial and Freight Transport (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation of certain industrial and freight transport lands and heritages (article 3) (“the prescribed class”) for the financial year 1992—93.

items.title: The Mines and Quarries (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1992
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation of certain mines and quarries (article 3) (“the prescribed class”) for the financial year 1992-93.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1992
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms 1990 by increasing the fees payable to Messengers-at-Arms by about 6%. The last increase was in the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1991 (S.I. 1991/291). It also amends the entry in the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1990 (S.I. 1990/379) relating to photocopies.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment) (Optional Procedure and Miscellaneous) 1992
The Revenue Support Grant (Specified Bodies) Regulations 1992
The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 6) Order 1992
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie above and between the Beryl, Linnhe and Ness oilfields south-east of the Shetland Islands.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.2) (Solicitors' Fees) 1992
The A16 Trunk Road (Great Grimsby Borough Boundary to Toll Bar Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 1990 Amendment Order 1992
The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition) (South Wight Borough Council) (Sports and Leisure) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations postpone, in the case of South Wight Borough Council, the requirement in the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) Regulations 1990 that the management of sports and leisure facilities be put out to competitive tendering. This requirement, which would otherwise take effect on 31st January 1992, is postponed to 1st January 1993.

items.title: The Clackmannan District Council (Vesting of Central Regional Council Land) (Scotland) Order 1991
items.description: Section 77 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 provides for the making of an Order vesting in public sector landlords land of which such landlords are not the heritable proprietors, where such vesting is necessary to enable tenants to exercise their rights to purchase their dwellinghouses. This Order vests in Clackmannan District Council as landlords certain land in the County of Clackmannan belonging to Central Regional Council in order to enable the District Council’s tenants to exercise their rights to purchase their dwelling houses.

items.title: The Control of Dogs Order 1992
items.description: This Order replaces with amendments the Control of Dogs Order 1930 concerning wearing of collars by dogs (article 2), offences (article 3), seizure of dogs (article 4) and enforcement (article 5). Changes of substance are—

items.title: The Palace of Westminster (Appointed Day) Order 1992
The Farm Woodland Premium Scheme 1992
items.description: This Scheme, which applies to Great Britain, provides for the payment of annual grants to abate financial losses incurred in consequence of the conversion of agricultural land (including, in Scotland, common grazings) to use for woodlands. The Scheme complies with Title VIII of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2328/91 on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures (OJ No. L218, 6.8.91 p.1). This Scheme supersedes the Farm Woodland Scheme 1988 under which no further applications may be accepted after the coming into force of this Scheme (paragraph 16).

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Leicestershire) Order 1992
The Dart Valley Light Railway Plc (Totnes and Ashburton) Light Railway (Transfer) Order 1992
The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) Order 1992
items.description: This Order increases the fees to be charged in England and Wales under the Gaming Act 1968 for the matters mentioned in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1992
The Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: These Regulations substitute a new Schedule 3 to the Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) Regulations 1992 which provide for the enforcement of EEC Regulations concerned with the production and marketing of wine and wine related products. The new Schedule 3 includes the vine varieties Madeleine angevine and Siegerrebe in the list of vine varieties to be used for the production of table wine bearing the geographical designation of any part of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The (A50) Nottingham–Derby–Stoke-on-Trent Trunk Road (Blythe Bridge to Queensway and Connecting Roads) Order 1992
The Lotteries (Gaming Board Fees) Order 1992
The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment Regulations 1992
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are made consequential upon amendments made to the Social Security Act 1975 (c. 14) by the Social Security (Contributions) Act 1991 (c. 42) (which introduced Class 1A contributions). This instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of that Act on 25th July 1991. The Regulations are therefore exempt, by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) as amended by the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24), Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3), from reference to the Social Security Advisory Committee and have not been so referred.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Prohibition of Flights) Order 1992
The Libya (United Nations Prohibition of Flights) (Dependent Territories) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, implements a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No.748 of 31st March 1992 by prohibiting aircraft from taking off from, landing in or overflying the territories specified in the Schedule if they are destined for, or have taken off from, Libya.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuantto a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No.748 of31st March 1992, on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libyaof arms and related materiel. It also places restrictions on arrangements for theuse of intellectual property rights by persons connected with Libya in connection withthe manufacture or maintenance of arms and related goods. It restricts the provision topersons connected with Libya of technical advice, assistance and training in relationto the supply, manufacture, maintenance or use of arms and related goods. The Order imposes restrictions on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of aircraft and aircraft components, and on the servicing of Libyan aircraft. It places restrictions on the insurance of such aircraft and prohibits persons from carrying on business as Libyan Arab Airlines.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule III. It imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 748 of 31st March 1992, on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of arms and related materiel. It also places restrictions on arrangements for the use of intellectual property rights by persons connected with Libya in connection with the manufacture or maintenance of arms and related goods. It restricts the provision to persons connected with Libya of technical advice, assistance and training in relation to the supply, manufacture, maintenance or use of arms and related goods. The Order imposes restrictions on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of aircraft and aircraft components, and on the servicing of Libyan aircraft. It places restrictions on the insurance of such aircraft and prohibits persons from carrying on business as Libyan Arab Airlines.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1992
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes in the Channel Islands restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No.748 of 31st March 1992, on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of arms and related materiel. It also places restrictions on arrangements for the use of intellectual property rights by persons connected with Libya in connection with the manufacture or maintenance of arms and related goods. It restricts the provision to persons connected with Libya of technical advice, assistance and training in relation to the supply, manufacture, maintenance or use of arms and related goods. The Order imposes restrictions on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of aircraft and aircraft components and on the servicing of Libyan aircraft. It places restrictions on the insurance of such aircraft and prohibits persons from carrying on business as Libyan Arab Airlines.

items.title: The Newcastle and Gateshead and Sunderland and South Shields Water (Amendment of Local Enactments) Order 1992
items.description: This Order amends or repeals local statutory provisions for the purposes of the replacement of Newcastle and Gateshead Water plc as water undertaker by Sunderland and South Shields Water plc.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Amendment) Regulations 1992
items.description: (This not is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1992
The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) (No. 2) Order 1992
items.description: This Order is the second order made under section 2(1) of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991; the Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1992, which was erroneously made before the coming into force of section 2(9) of the Act, is revoked.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 1992 (revoked)
items.description: This Order specifies the fees payable for the year beginning 1st April 1992 under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters.

items.title: The Shetland Islands Area (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1992
The Highland Regional Council (Abhainn Mhungasdail) Water Order 1992
